title: NASA's Spacecraft Crash Changes an Asteroid's Path
summary: NASA crashed a spacecraft called DART into a small asteroid named Dimorphos in 2022. This was a test to see if we could move an asteroid to protect Earth. The crash worked! It changed how Dimorphos moves around its bigger partner, Didymos. Scientists found that it also changed the path of both asteroids around the Sun a tiny bit. This is the first time humans have changed the orbit of a space object around the Sun. The crash blasted rocks into space, which gave an extra push to the asteroids. Even a small change can grow over time to make an asteroid miss Earth. This shows we might be able to stop dangerous asteroids from hitting our planet in the future.

background_read: Asteroids are rocky objects that orbit the Sun, mostly found between Mars and Jupiter. Some asteroids come close to Earth and could be dangerous if they hit our planet. NASA's DART mission was a test to see if crashing a spacecraft into an asteroid could change its path to protect Earth. This is called planetary defense. The crash happened in 2022, and scientists have been studying it to learn how to keep Earth safe from space rocks.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Crashed a spacecraft into an asteroid to test if we can move it
WHERE: In space, around the asteroid Dimorphos
WHY: To protect Earth from dangerous asteroids in the future

perspectives.description: This was the first time humans changed an asteroid's orbit around the Sun, showing we can use technology to defend Earth.
